item.title: Day 14 - Ukiah to San Francisco, CA
item.description:
I’m home! The last day was a big push. It ended up being my second longest day of the trip at 132 miles.

I got up at 5am and Joe had already made me an awesome breakfast of pancakes, eggs, bacon and hash browns. We packed up and hit …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Day 12/13 - Klamath to Laytonville, CA
item.description: No update yesterday. Wasn't too much to report back. It was a good day of riding. I had to stop fairly early and get breakfast since Klamath had no restaurant or store. The redwoods were amazing. I got lucky and got to go ahead of a long line of cars …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Day 11 - Port Orford to Klamath, CA
item.description:
Today was foggy. All day. Fortunately it wasn't too cold.

This picture says it all. I'm back in California! The hardest part now is seeing all the road signs reminding me how many miles I have left. In some crazy fantasy, I keep thinking "well if I ride all day …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Day 10 - Florence to Port Orford, OR
item.description:
Today was a nice day to ride. While there was a ridiculous amount of traffic, the shoulders were wide and the pavement was really smooth. It was mostly flat with some gradual climbs but I managed to ride 103 miles in just over 6 hours.

Having the ocean to my …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Day 9 - Eugene to Florence, OR
item.description:
Today was the first day that really felt like a slog and work to get the miles in.

There was far more traffic than any other day so far. The scenery wasn't bad, but nothing too interesting. No big climbs, no descents. Just nothing really worth enjoying for the day …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Day 8 - Sisters to Eugene, OR
item.description:
I think today may have been the prettiest ride I've done to date. My morning started off by stopping at a bike shop and getting proper tubes and a new pair of socks. I then went to Sisters Coffee Company and grabbed some breakfast.

The ride began with a fairly …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Day 7 - Mitchell to Sisters, OR
item.description:
Today might have been one of my best days yet. The hostel I stayed at was fantastic. It was in an old church that they had converted. The hosts were very nice and treated us to great food, showers and comfy beds.

Since I had ridden so far yesterday, I …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Day 6 - Baker City to Mitchell, OR
item.description: The hostel I stayed at was just amazing. Nice cozy bed, laundry, beer and stone fruit from the tree outside. I was so tired last night that I couldn't keep my eyes open at 8pm. I knew if I went to sleep that early, I could probably get up early …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Day 5 - Oxford to Baker City, OR
item.description: The morning was by far the best part of my day. A bunch of people came over to see what I was up to and check out my bike. The people camping next to me offered to make me breakfast and hang out with them. I couldn't say no to …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Day 4 - New Meadows, ID to Oxbow, OR
item.description:
The day started off getting a ride up to McCall to get my bike fixed. Everything went very well there. The repair was super cheap and the guy even did an alcohol rub on my back rotor and cleaned my chain.

The ride started by riding the 10 miles back …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Bike repairs
item.description: I stayed in a very nice BnB last night. The lady at the front desk mentioned that the owner may be going to McCall, ID in the morning where a bike shop would be. I met up with him this morning and he was willing to give me a ride …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Day 3 - Kooskia to New Meadows, ID
item.description:
Today was my first solo day of the trip. Everything started out more-or-less fine. There was a really long and steep climb in the first 10 miles. It was tough but it was followed by a great 7 mile descent.

Unfortunately at the bottom of the hill things changed a …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Day 2 - Lochsa to Kooskia, ID
item.description: The day started out cloudy but was relatively nice. About 20 miles in we started getting rain that lasted about an hour. Once the rain stopped the clouds started clearing out and we ended up having sun for the rest of the day. All in all we couldn't have asked …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Day 1 - Rain
item.description:
I left a day early since I knew rain was coming and I wouldn't have to ride quite as far. Turned out to be one of my best ideas ever. Within the first 10 minutes of riding the rain started and continued throughout the entire day.

Before riding, I stopped …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: The Start
item.description:
I'm starting a day early since rain has been forecast for the next couple of days. I want to get a head start and not do as many miles the first two days. My plan is to ride about 60-70 miles today and stay at Lochsa Lodge in Idaho.

Stopped …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Packing up the bike
item.description: I'm using BikeFlights.com to ship my bike. I had bought a TriSport Velo Safe box a year ago since I had planned on doing PBP. I'd never packed or flown with a bike before and so I asked my friend Joe to help me out and make sure that …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Gear Setup
item.description:
I’ve gotten a few questions about the bags and gear that I’m using so I figured it’s worth taking a few pictures and listing off what I’m going to be using during the ride.

Bike

I was riding the Volagi Viaje XL (steel) for the last …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Missoula to San Francisco: The Route
item.description:
My plan is to depart on September 7 from Missoula, MT. I’ll be following the TransAmerica route all the way to the Oregon coast where I’ll meet up with the Pacific Coast Trail and ride south all the way to San Francisco.

My hope is that I can …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M

item.title: Getting ready for the ride
item.description: I had some free time over the weekend to see how much gear I could get on my bike. It was a good chance to see what I already had and what I still needed to buy for the trip. I already had the seat bag, and the frame bag …
item.dc:creator: Ryan M
